item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This made me imagine teeth growing faster and faster until they just snipe important parts of various ecosystems at mach 9 or something.

Nat Geo voice: "And here we see the hedgehog, eating insects which otherwise would flood the fore-splat- HOLY ####! DID YOU SEE THAT?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am physically uncomfortable, take your upvote +1
each upvote to this is another tooth in my collection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upon hearing of SCP-4667 and its anomalous effects, Stanford requested for SCP-4667 to be relocated his office in the United Kingdom.

Shouldn't that be "relocated to his office"?

Otherwise, this is really good and does TEETH very well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oops, those were artifacts from a previous iteration of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love the article, +1, but one thing keeps irking me. You refer to the logs multiple times as 'audio logs', but shouldn't they be video? You describe a lot of physical things, and mention a camera being destroyed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: heh, yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
primarily expire via tooth growths elongating through vital organisms,

is that supposed to say organs?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly think I like that aspect given its the classic "the humanoid is being described" syndrome. It's like Christ the symbol rather than Christ the actual person imo, which is a really fun persona to play with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh spendid, you posted Toothulhu!

I always love some good body horror. Just as great of a read as it was during drafting. Great job lad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Teeth for the teeth god!

I absolutely loved the imagery of 4667 rising through the floor on that column, talk about making an entrance.

Everything with Wendell was just :O in the best way.

+1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you!

My only quibble is that 4667 doesn’t really have the opportunity to be a character in her own article, we just get the characters of the men speaking for her. I think that you could do more with the toothless enamel sculptor than lock her in a room to remotely torment her captors. Flying off into the sunset at the end is fun but maybe give her a little more color before that.

Yeah, it was a bit hard giving showing the character I wanted her to have due to her being mute (for a specific reason which I may expand on in later works), which is a bit hard to do in a written work, especially when the character doesn't know sign language, and when the character isn't visible all that often. I'm glad it didn't subtract too much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
HATE this.

+1

Anything with teeth gets me and combining that with this absurd but following it’s own logic type of article is great stuff. I’m glad I didn’t read this before going to bed last night.

My only quibble is that 4667 doesn’t really have the opportunity to be a character in her own article, we just get the characters of the men speaking for her. I think that you could do more with the toothless enamel sculptor than lock her in a room to remotely torment her captors. Flying off into the sunset at the end is fun but maybe give her a little more color before that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i’m not saying they weren’t in use at all, I’m saying they were prominently used outside of Cold War spying (in the case of sky drones at least) and other military reasons. I also gotta feel that just pulling one D-class’s (I’m going to change that to singular when i’m actually at a computer) teeth is a fine alternative to using an expensive drone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry, but remote control ground vehicles were first developed in the 1930s

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article in-universe was written during the 80s, before the use of drones was prominent. I hint at this a bit throughout, all of the photos are old looking, they say “USSR” instead of Russia, everything is dated pre-80s, etc etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is, without question, the most horrifying teeth-related article I've ever read on the wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dear diary, today I ordered a half dozen people's teeth pulled because I forgot drones exist.

Seriously while I appreciate the lurid containment procedure, it doesn't hold up to scrutiny…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a job for Lemming of the BDA.

"Build me a sculpture, Danny"

"Of what my sweet?"

"Of me!"

"Shall I make it of marble, or wood? Or maybe something a little more expensive?"

"Teeth"

"I… what?"

"TEETH"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A tasty crunchy treat for inbetween.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of the teddy bear, but teeth.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I know this'll be compared to literally any other SCP about teeth, but I don't care, I had a ton of fun writing it.

Thanks to Deadly Bread, and KnightAlaska for reading the teeth.

Image Sources
– hide block

All are Wikimedia Commons.

TEETH: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cleidocranial_dysplasia_teeth.jpg
Ivadente: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:%C8%98tefan_Luchian_-_%C8%9Aiganc%C4%83_(drawing,_ca._1893-1902).jpg
Russ: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:De_uit_Griekenland_uitgewezen_Nederlandse_journalist_Frans_van_Hasselt_geeft_per,_Bestanddeelnr_091-0484.jpg
Mavis: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Narcyz_Witczak-Witaczy%C5%84ski_-_Wycieczka_podoficer%C3%B3w_Wojska_Polskiego_na_Pomorze_(107-164-23).jpg
Iosif: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Athanasios_Douroutis.jpg
Dentists: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bolsheviks_Kharkov_Ukraine_anrc_04262a.jpg
